ddls:
CREATE TABLE "pricing_records" (
  "pricing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pricing record */,
  "time_generated" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pricing record was generated */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the services table */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  "currency_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the currencies table */,
  "meter_name" TEXT /* Name of the meter (service component) for which the pricing is applicable */,
  "tier" TEXT /* Tier of the commitment (e.g., Pay-as-you-go, 100 GB, 200 GB) */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measure for the pricing (e.g., 1 GB, 1/Day) */,
  "retail_price" REAL /* Retail price for the service */,
  "effective_start_date" TEXT /* Effective start date of the pricing */,
  "effective_commitment_tier_threshold_gb" REAL /* Threshold in GB for the commitment tier */,
  "effective_price_per_gb" REAL /* Price per GB for the commitment tier */,
  "commitment_period" TEXT /* Commitment period (e.g., 1 year, 3 years) */,
  "min_commitment_gb" REAL /* Minimum commitment in GB */,
  "max_commitment_gb" REAL /* Maximum commitment in GB */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the pricing record is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pricing record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the pricing record was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pricing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pricing_records_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pricing_records_currency_id FOREIGN KEY ("currency_id") REFERENCES currencies ("currency_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_pricing_records_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "service_name" TEXT /* Name of the service */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the service */,
  "service_category" TEXT /* Category of the service (e.g., analytics, storage) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the service is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the service was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the Azure region */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region */,
  "geo_region" TEXT /* Geographic region (e.g., North America, Europe) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the region is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the region was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the region was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "currencies" (
  "currency_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each currency */,
  "currency_code" TEXT /* Currency code (e.g., CAD, USD) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the currency */,
  "exchange_rate" REAL /* Exchange rate with respect to the base currency */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the currency is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the currency was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the currency was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("currency_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "commitment_tiers" (
  "tier_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each commitment tier */,
  "tier_name" TEXT /* Name of the commitment tier (e.g., Pay-as-you-go, 100 GB, 200 GB) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the commitment tier */,
  "min_commitment_gb" REAL /* Minimum commitment in GB */,
  "max_commitment_gb" REAL /* Maximum commitment in GB */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the commitment tier is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the commitment tier was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the commitment tier was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tier_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "meters" (
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each meter */,
  "meter_name" TEXT /* Name of the meter (service component) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the meter */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measure for the meter (e.g., 1 GB, 1/Day) */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the meter is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the meter was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the meter was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("meter_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pricing_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each historical pricing record */,
  "pricing_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the pricing records table */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Effective date of the historical pricing */,
  "retail_price" REAL /* Retail price for the historical pricing */,
  "effective_commitment_tier_threshold_gb" REAL /* Threshold in GB for the commitment tier for the historical pricing */,
  "effective_price_per_gb" REAL /* Price per GB for the commitment tier for the historical pricing */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the historical pricing was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer usage record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the customers table */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the services table */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the regions table */,
  "meter_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the meters table */,
  "usage_date" TEXT /* Date of the customer usage */,
  "usage_amount" REAL /* Amount of usage by the customer */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer usage was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_usage_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_usage_meter_id FOREIGN KEY ("meter_id") REFERENCES meters ("meter_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_usage_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_usage_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "customer_name" TEXT /* Name of the customer */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the customer */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the customer is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)
